reviewed_items.reviewer_notes: Verified code at types/src/config.rs L1044-1046 and network constructors (sepolia L512-520, holesky L637-645, hoodi L739-747). The blob_schedule field uses serde attribute `sorted_list_desc_by_key` (L164) which deserializes to descending order (serde_utils/src/sorted_list_desc_by_key.rs L49), but hardcoded network constructors define entries in ascending order. The selector uses find_map which returns the FIRST match, so with ascending data it returns the lowest qualifying epoch instead of highest. For Sepolia at epoch 280000, this returns entry (274_176, max_blobs=15) instead of correct entry (275_712, max_blobs=21). Validation at fulu/block_processing.rs L186-194 enforces this incorrect limit. An attacker can trigger consensus failure by proposing valid blocks with 16-21 blobs on the P2P network, causing nodes running Grandine to reject them while spec-compliant clients accept them. This creates a network split affecting all Grandine nodes. Severity: Medium per BUG_BOUNTY_SCOPE - affects >5% of network if Grandine market share exceeds that threshold. Spec reference from 01e_PARTIAL_W0B1_1771748647.json confirms requirement: 'get_blob_parameters sorted schedule must select the highest-epoch entry not exceeding the query epoch' (assertion: forall epoch e: get_blob_parameters(e) == entry with max(entry.EPOCH) where entry.EPOCH <= e).
reviewed_items.spec_reference: get_blob_parameters sorted schedule must select the highest-epoch entry not exceeding the query epoch. Assertion: forall epoch e: get_blob_parameters(e) == entry with max(entry.EPOCH) where entry.EPOCH <= e
